content:
# Carbonate Geology

**Summary**: The study of carbonate rocks and their properties in petroleum systems.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[1992-rodriguez|Rodriguez, 1992]] focused on the mineralogy and surface chemistry of carbonates.

- [[1992-el-sahn|El-Sahn, 1992]] applied carbonate-geology to analyze the Dammam and Asmari formations.

- [[2011-busubait|Busubait, 2011]] applied carbonate-geology to understand the sedimentary and stratigraphic controls on reservoir quality.

- [[2012-jabbar|JABBAR, 2012]] focused on the carbonate geology aspect of wettibility alteration for EOR.

- [[2012-kassim|KASSIM, 2012]] studied carbonate-geology to evaluate the behavior of limestone under various chemical conditions.

- [[2012-elhaj|Elhaj, 2012]] focused on the carbonate geology of the Dam Formation to improve reservoir characterization.

- [[2013-abdalla|Abdalla, 2013]] applied carbonate geology principles to analyze the Arab-D reservoir.

- [[2013-khan|Khan, 2013]] focused on carbonate-geology specifically regarding the impact of high pressure on wormholing.

- [[2013-kareem|Kareem, 2013]] Referenced in this thesis.

- [[2014-jangda|Jangda, 2014]] addressed the specific challenges of carbonate-reservoirs in high salinity environments.

- [[2014-kasha|Kasha, 2014]] focused on the behavior of carbonate minerals (calcite, dolomite) in reservoir environments.

- [[2014-al-ameri|AL-AMERI, 2014]] focused on the behavior of limestone and dolomite under CO2 injection.

- [[2015-koeshidayatullah|Koeshidayatullah, 2015]] focused on carbonate-geology to model the development of platforms in a rift setting.

- [[2015-ali|Ali, 2015]] investigated the Dam Formation as a mixed carbonate-siliciclastic system.

- [[2016-hassan-2|Hassan, 2016]] analyzed the carbonate-sedimentology of the Dam Formation.

- [[2016-bashri|Bashri, 2016]] investigated the carbonate-geology of the Dam Formation.

- [[2016-salih|Salih, 2016]] focused on the carbonate geology of the Dam Formation to understand its evolution.

- [[2016-radwan|Radwan, 2016]] focused on carbonate-geology to understand the diagenetic history of the Tuwaiq Mountain Formation.

- [[2016-makkawi|Makkawi, 2016]] focused on the carbonate geology of the Khuff Formation.

- [[2017-sadeed|SADEED, 2017]] focused on the chemistry of carbonate surfaces to improve oil recovery.

- [[2017-elyamani|ElYamani, 2017]] focused on the carbonate-geology of the Tuwaiq Mountain Formation to evaluate its potential as a reservoir.

- [[2017-mahrous|Mahrous, 2017]] Referenced in this thesis.

- [[2017-adam-2|Adam, 2017]] investigated the carbonate systems of the Khuff formation.

- [[2017-ibrahim|Ibrahim, 2017]] Referenced in this thesis.

- [[2018-ismanto|Ismanto, 2018]] focused on the carbonate geology of the Dhruma Formation.

- [[2020-al-zoukani|Al-Zoukani, 2020]] focused on carbonate-geology to characterize the Dhruma Formation.

- [[2020-aly|Aly, 2020]] focused on the carbonate-geology of Saudi limestone for acidizing studies.

- [[2021-al-nuwais|Al-Nuwais, 2021]] focused on the carbonate geology of Jurassic formations in Saudi Arabia.

- [[2021-albensaad|Albensaad, 2021]] investigated the carbonate mudrock system in the eastern basin.

- [[2021-alabyadh|Alabyadh, 2021]] investigated the carbonate geology of the Monte Sacro sequence to understand reservoir potential.

- [[2022-syahputra|Syahputra, 2022]] investigated the carbonate_geology of ramp systems.

- [[2022-samarkin|Samarkin, 2022]] focused on the behavior of various chemicals in carbonate rock systems.

- [[2022-stiawan|Stiawan, 2022]] focused on carbonate-geology to understand the reservoir properties of the Jubaila Formation.

- [[2022-salih|Salih, 2022]] studied various carbonate formations to understand the impact of different 'factories' on velocity.

- [[2022-alsulaiman|AlSulaiman, 2022]] investigated the impact of mineralogy (limestone vs. dolomite) on acid fracturing outcomes in carbonate-geology.

- [[2022-al-sadah|Al-Sadah, 2022]] focused on carbonate-geology to evaluate the Miocene sequences in the Umm Lajj Quadrangle.

- [[2022-kalam|Kalam, 2022]] investigated the behavior of surfactants on carbonate rocks.

- [[2023-al-garni|Al-Garni, 2023]] Referenced in this thesis.

- [[2023-al-boghail|AL-BOGHAIL, 2023]] focused on the challenges posed by diffusion coupling in carbonate formations.

- [[2024-allam|Allam, 2024]] investigated the carbonate geology of Valanginian carbonates.

- [[2023-pratama|PRATAMA, 2023]] Referenced in this thesis.

- [[2023-desouky|Desouky, 2023]] focused on carbonate-geology to evaluate acid treatment in limestone and chalk.

- [[2024-saraih|Saraih, 2024]] applied carbonate-geology principles to evaluate the impact of burrow morphology on reservoir quality.

- [[2024-al-azani|Al-Azani, 2024]] investigated the behavior of surfactants in carbonate reservoirs.

- [[2024-hassan|Hassan, 2024]] Referenced in this thesis.

- [[2024-fahmi|Fahmi, 2024]] investigated the carbonate-geology of the Marrat Formation to understand its heterogeneity.

- [[2024-muharrag|Muharrag, 2024]] Referenced in this thesis.

- [[2024-allehyani|allehyani, 2024]] focused on the geology of carbonate rocks in the Hanifa Formation.

- [[2025-alhubail|Alhubail, 2025]] focused on the carbonate-geology of mudrocks in response to CO2 treatment.

- [[1992-rodriguez-2|Rodriguez, 1992]] investigated the behavior of surfactants on carbonate rock surfaces.

- [[1992-abdulghani|Abdulghani, 1992]] focused on the carbonate-geology of the Arab-D Limestone Member.

## Methods and Approaches Used

- [[1992-rodriguez|Rodriguez, 1992]] ['zeta-potential-measurement', 'adsorption-experiments']

## Key Findings Across Sources

- The isoelectric point was found to be 4.2 in deionized water. ([[1992-rodriguez|Rodriguez, 1992, p. 50]])

- the study... has been proved to represent a steadily shallowing upward carbonate cycle. ([[1992-el-sahn|El-Sahn, 1992, p. 78]])

- The Arab-C is a primary example of an upward-shoaling carbonate to evaporate cycle. ([[2011-busubait|Busubait, 2011, p. 34]])

- Carbonate reservoirs often have a neutral to oil-wet character due to the adsorption of carboxylic acids. ([[2012-jabbar|JABBAR, 2012, p. 16]])

- Limestone showed distinct adsorption behaviors for different ions depending on temperature and crude oil presence. ([[2012-kassim|KASSIM, 2012, p. 103]])

- Carbonate reservoirs are highly heterogeneous at all scales, making them difficult to characterize. ([[2012-elhaj|Elhaj, 2012, p. 16]])

- Identified several lithofacies associations interpreted as a gentle slope platform. ([[2013-abdalla|Abdalla, 2013, p. 18]])

- Carbonate rocks exhibit distinct wormhole growth patterns based on acid flux and mineralogy. ([[2013-khan|Khan, 2013, p. 23]])

- Referenced in this thesis. ([[2013-kareem|Kareem, 2013]])

- Indiana Limestone was used to simulate carbonate reservoir conditions. ([[2014-jangda|Jangda, 2014, p. 57]])

- Investigated how different ions affect the surface chemistry of calcite and dolomite. ([[2014-kasha|Kasha, 2014, p. 73]])

- Identified that high carbonate content makes rock more prone to mechanical weakening. ([[2014-al-ameri|AL-AMERI, 2014, p. 51]])

- Identified three distinct architectural models for carbonate platforms based on tectonic settings. ([[2015-koeshidayatullah|Koeshidayatullah, 2015, p. 19]])

- The study highlights how facies heterogeneity impacts reservoir quality in carbonate systems. ([[2015-ali|Ali, 2015, p. 17]])

- The Dam Formation is a Middle Miocene Formation composed of mixed carbonate-siliciclastic sediments. ([[2016-hassan-2|Hassan, 2016, p. 73]])

- Over 80% of the facies are carbonates, ranging from subtidal to supratidal environments. ([[2016-bashri|Bashri, 2016, p. 120]])

- Identified 15 lithofacies within a carbonate ramp setting. ([[2016-salih|Salih, 2016, p. 16]])

- The study identified two main diagenetic processes: chertification and calcitization. ([[2016-radwan|Radwan, 2016, p. 14]])

- The reservoir is characterized by oolitic grainstones, packstones, and wackestones in a ramp setting. ([[2016-makkawi|Makkawi, 2016, p. 46]])

- Carbonate reservoirs exhibit 60% of the world oil reserves and are often characterized as low porosity and naturally fractured. ([[2017-sadeed|SADEED, 2017, p. 24]])

- Identified various lagoonal and shoal environments within the TMF. ([[2017-elyamani|ElYamani, 2017, p. 87]])

- Referenced in this thesis. ([[2017-mahrous|Mahrous, 2017]])

- Identified oolitic, peloidal, and bioclastic lithofacies. ([[2017-adam-2|Adam, 2017, p. 81]])

- Referenced in this thesis. ([[2017-ibrahim|Ibrahim, 2017]])

- The Dhruma Formation is a good example of an ancient carbonate ramp to be studied. ([[2018-ismanto|Ismanto, 2018, p. 16]])

- 15 lithofacies were identified based on Dunham's classification for carbonate rocks. ([[2020-al-zoukani|Al-Zoukani, 2020, p. 69]])

- Saudi carbonate rocks were used as the primary material for both linear and radial acidizing experiments. ([[2020-aly|Aly, 2020, p. 22]])

- Jurassic carbonates are important targets for hydrocarbon reservoirs, source rocks, and unconventional resources. ([[2021-al-nuwais|Al-Nuwais, 2021, p. 13]])

- Calcite is the dominant mineral, and its concentration correlates positively with rock hardness. ([[2021-albensaad|Albensaad, 2021, p. 73]])

- Carbonate rocks are essential for hydrocarbon storage, with 60% of global reserves in carbonates. ([[2021-alabyadh|Alabyadh, 2021, p. 15]])

- Burrowed strata are common in middle carbonate ramps and serve as important flow zones. ([[2022-syahputra|Syahputra, 2022, p. 17]])

- Different carbonates (chalk, limestone, dolomite) showed varying responses to chemical treatments. ([[2022-samarkin|Samarkin, 2022, p. 13]])

- Carbonate reservoirs are complex due to variety of depositional textures and diagenetic overprints. ([[2022-stiawan|Stiawan, 2022, p. 18]])

- Identified distinct behaviors in carbonates based on their formation history (e.g., Khuff vs. Hanifa). ([[2022-salih|Salih, 2022, p. 25]])

- Dolomite shows a bell-shaped conductivity profile due to its sensitivity to temperature during acid reaction. ([[2022-alsulaiman|AlSulaiman, 2022, p. 40]])

- The carbonates were identified as part of a distal reef complex under deeper conditions (<200 m). ([[2022-al-sadah|Al-Sadah, 2022, p. 76]])

- Carbonates were found to have lower surfactant adsorption compared to sandstones due to their positive surface charge. ([[2022-kalam|Kalam, 2022, p. 58]])

- Referenced in this thesis. ([[2023-al-garni|Al-Garni, 2023]])

- Complex carbonate systems result in inaccurate assessment of the reservoir medium when diffusion coupling is present. ([[2023-al-boghail|AL-BOGHAIL, 2023, p. 101]])

- The carbonates are mainly formed of calcite minerals, with dolomitization playing a minor role. ([[2024-allam|Allam, 2024, p. 72]])

- Referenced in this thesis. ([[2023-pratama|PRATAMA, 2023]])

- Acid etching significantly reduced sample stiffness by up to 80% in some cases. ([[2023-desouky|Desouky, 2023, p. 113]])

- Identified three distinct BIFMs based on different depositional environments and diagenetic histories. ([[2024-saraih|Saraih, 2024, p. 57]])

- Carbonate rocks are characterized by high heterogeneity and complex pore structures. ([[2024-al-azani|Al-Azani, 2024, p. 136]])

- Referenced in this thesis. ([[2024-hassan|Hassan, 2024]])

- The Marrat Formation is an ideal mixed carbonate-siliciclastic system developed under transgressive-regressive sequence. ([[2024-fahmi|Fahmi, 2024, p. 16]])

- Referenced in this thesis. ([[2024-muharrag|Muharrag, 2024]])

- Carbonate rocks are highly heterogeneous due to diverse depositional environments and diagenetic processes. ([[2024-allehyani|allehyani, 2024, p. 24]])

- Carbonate mudrocks are a primary target for carbon sequestration due to their high porosity and permeability. ([[2025-alhubail|Alhubail, 2025, p. 10]])

- The specific surface area of the rock chips was 0.317 m²/s. ([[1992-rodriguez-2|Rodriguez, 1992, p. 43]])

- The Arab-D Limestone Member is a major carbonate reservoir with high oil productivity associated with calcarenites. ([[1992-abdulghani|Abdulghani, 1992, p. 40]])

## Competing Approaches and Debates

- [[1992-rodriguez|Rodriguez, 1992]] Complexity of the interfacial region between carbonates and brine.
